research_gaps: SRL audit reports not publicly readable from GitHub repos — only audited code is posted.
Entity structure beyond EoT Labs GmbH unclear — possible foundation entity not identified.
Exact X/Twitter follower count not confirmed.
Revenue is minimal — $163.6K annualised fees per DeFiLlama. No protocol revenue dashboard found.
TVL very low ($726K) for an L1 chain — DeFi is not primary use case.
HuggingFace presence checked — 0 public models or datasets.
No Binance main listing — only Binance Alpha.
Total supply after inflation since November 2024 launch not precisely calculated.
Insider vesting completion dates not fully documented beyond duration periods.
CoinList ICO ($20M) may overlap with the private sale allocation — exact breakdown unclear.

identity.name: peaq
identity.tagline: The blockchain for the Machine Economy
identity.one_liner: Layer-1 blockchain purpose-built for DePIN, enabling machines, vehicles, robots, and IoT devices to function as economic participants.

founding_team.name: Till Wendler
founding_team.background: Tech startup background, introduced co-founders to blockchain in 2015 in Berlin.

founding_team.name: Leonard Dorlochter
founding_team.background: Entered blockchain in Berlin's early crypto community circa 2015. Previously delivered blockchain solutions for Audi.

founding_team.name: Max Thake
founding_team.background: Built Advanced Blockchain AG, the first blockchain development company listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange. Graduated from MCAST (Malta).

rounds.lead_investor: Werner Geissler (ex-P&G VP), Michael Ganser (ex-Cisco Germany CEO)

rounds.notes: With Spartan Group, HV Capital, CMCC Global, Animoca Brands, Moonrock Capital

technical.architecture_summary: Substrate-based Layer-1 blockchain running as a Polkadot parachain. Dual smart contract support: EVM (Solidity) and WASM (Rust/ink!). Native machine-economy modules built into the chain: peaq ID (machine identity), peaq access (RBAC), peaq verify (data verification), peaq store (key-value storage), peaq pay (M2M payments), UMT (time sync). 49K TPS achieved in Q3 2025 with target of 100K+ TPS.
technical.consensus_mechanism: Delegated Proof of Stake (DPoS) for block production + Nominated Proof of Stake (NPoS) for verification. Secured by Polkadot Relay Chain.
technical.what_is_onchain: Machine identities (DIDs), access control, data verification, M2M payments, staking, governance. Full EVM and WASM smart contract execution.
technical.what_is_offchain: Physical machine operations, sensor data collection, IoT device communication. Data flows on-chain via peaq verify/store modules.

audits.scope: Core chain pallets (DID, MOR, RBAC, Transaction, Storage)
audits.result_summary: Score 97.96/100 on foundational health. 35 findings: 0 critical, 4 major, 9 medium, 8 minor, 14 informational. Ranked #1 on CertiK Skynet for pre-launch projects.
Audit completed. Full report not publicly readable from GitHub repos.

token_utility: Gas fees for transactions on peaq L1
Staking for network security (DPoS/NPoS validators and delegators)

initial_allocation.notes: 357M tokens for the entity behind peaq

distribution.distribution_method: Private rounds + CoinList ICO + community airdrops + staking emissions

emissions.halving_schedule: Inflation decreases 10% annually until reaching 1% (~12 years post-launch)

staking.lock_up_period: Variable — locked tokens can be staked
staking_risks: Delegator risk if chosen validator is slashed
APY varies 5-15% depending on total staked

tokenomics.token_model_classification: Utility token for gas fees, staking, governance, machine identity, and M2M payments on a purpose-built DePIN L1. Disinflationary with 3.5% annual inflation decreasing to 1%.
tokenomics.tokenomics_notes: Genesis supply of 4.2B with no max supply. 44.3% circulating. Insider allocation (Core Team 11.5% + EoT Labs 8.5% + Pre-Seed 7% + Seed 5% + Private Sale 13% = 45%) is significant. Inflation model is well-structured — 3.5% decreasing 10% annually to 1%. 40% of inflation goes to validators/delegators. Token down 98% from ATH. FDV of $64.7M is reasonable relative to $24.9M raised but investors from higher-priced rounds may be significantly underwater.

methods.description: Delegate PEAQ tokens to validators to secure the network. Earn staking rewards from inflation distribution (40% of inflation goes to validators/delegators).
Run a collator or validator node on the peaq network. Collators create blocks; validators ensure finalisation via Polkadot Relay Chain.
barriers_to_entry: Technical expertise in Substrate node operation

methods.description: Build DePIN applications on peaq using native machine-economy modules (peaq ID, access, verify, store, pay) and EVM/WASM smart contracts. SDKs available in JavaScript, Python, and Rust.
methods.estimated_returns: Ecosystem grants available. 20% of inflation goes to DePIN Incentive Pool.

usage_metrics.revenue_source: Transaction fees on peaq L1 chain
usage_metrics.metrics_source: DeFiLlama for TVL and fees. peaq Machine Economy Report Q3 2025 for transaction counts.
usage_metrics.usage_notes: 60+ DePINs across 22 industries building on peaq. 6M+ people and machines on-chain (primarily via Teneo with 6M+ community nodes). 40.4M transactions in Q3 2025. TVL is low ($726K) because DeFi is not the primary use case — peaq is infrastructure for machine economy, not trading. Enterprise partnerships include NTT, Continental, Airbus, Mastercard, Bosch, Gaia. Real usage exists but monetisation is minimal — $163.6K annualised fees.

community.governance_model: On-chain governance via Substrate governance module. Token holders can propose and vote on protocol changes.
community.sentiment_summary: Active community with strong DePIN narrative. 60+ projects building on the chain creates ecosystem momentum. Enterprise partnerships (NTT, Continental, Airbus, Bosch) add credibility. Token price decline (98% from ATH) is a concern but the team continues shipping.
community.community_notes: Discord membership of 144K is strong for a project this size. Telegram has migrated activity to Discord. Developer community is active across 97 GitHub repos.

infrastructure_decentralisation.evidence: Polkadot parachain secured by Relay Chain validators. DPoS/NPoS with distributed collators and validators. 49K TPS achieved. However, as a parachain, peaq depends on Polkadot for security — not independently sovereign. Collator set size and distribution not documented. Enterprise focus (NTT, Bosch, Airbus) suggests some centralisation in key partnerships.

governance_decentralisation.evidence: Substrate governance module enables on-chain proposals and voting. Standard Polkadot-style governance. However, 45% insider allocation (team + investors) concentrates voting power. EoT Labs GmbH in Berlin controls development direction. Governance participation data not available.

token_distribution_fairness.evidence: 45% insider allocation: Core Team 11.5% + EoT Labs 8.5% + Pre-Seed 7% + Seed 5% + Private Sale 13%. Community-accessible allocation is 21% (Community Initiatives 15% + Launchpad 6%). Ecosystem & Treasury (20%) is team-controlled. Only 44.3% circulating after ~16 months.

censorship_resistance.evidence: Permissionless chain — anyone can deploy EVM or WASM contracts. Machine identities (DIDs) are self-sovereign. Polkadot Relay Chain provides shared security. However, Polkadot parachain dependency means peaq's liveness depends on maintaining a parachain slot. Enterprise integrations may introduce compliance requirements.

data_sovereignty.evidence: peaq ID provides self-sovereign machine identities (W3C DID compliant). peaq store enables verifiable machine data storage on-chain. Data verification via peaq verify. However, no privacy-preserving computation — data stored is visible on-chain. No encryption guarantees for machine data beyond what applications implement.

open_source_transparency.evidence: 97 public GitHub repos (41 original, 56 forks). Core chain node repo is 98.3% Rust, actively maintained with recent commits. Multiple audits from CertiK (97.96/100) and SRL. SDKs in JS, Python, Rust all open source. Good transparency for an L1 chain. Score limited because SRL audit reports are not publicly readable.

freedom_score.freedom_summary: peaq is a well-engineered open-source L1 with genuine infrastructure decentralisation via Polkadot. Strong audit history and active GitHub presence. However, 45% insider allocation, Polkadot parachain dependency, enterprise-focused partnerships, and lack of privacy features limit the sovereignty case. Freedom Score 53/100 (D) reflects a solid technical foundation with centralised control dynamics.

token_utility.evidence: PEAQ is used for gas fees, staking (DPoS/NPoS), governance, machine identity registration, and M2M payments. Multiple genuine utilities — the token is structurally essential to the chain. 60+ DePINs building on peaq use PEAQ for operations. Score reflects strong multi-utility design with real ecosystem usage.

value_accrual.evidence: Transaction fees are paid in PEAQ (gas). Staking rewards from inflation (40% of inflation to validators/delegators). 20% of inflation to DePIN Incentive Pool. No fee distribution to non-staking holders. No burn mechanism. Value accrual is primarily through staking and gas demand. With only $163.6K annualised fees, fee-based accrual is negligible currently.

supply_dynamics.evidence: No max supply but well-structured disinflationary model: 3.5% annual inflation decreasing 10% per year to 1%. 44.3% circulating after ~16 months is reasonable. Insider vesting is documented with cliffs and linear schedules. No burn mechanism. Score reflects a thoughtful emission schedule but meaningful inflation ongoing and 55.7% of genesis supply still locked.

revenue_sustainability.evidence: Real usage exists: 40.4M transactions in Q3 2025, 60+ DePINs, 6M+ machines/people on-chain. Enterprise partnerships (NTT, Continental, Airbus, Bosch) suggest commercial traction. But monetisation is minimal — $163.6K annualised fees. Revenue does not come close to covering emissions. Early-stage revenue generation with real activity but not yet sustainable.

liquidity_access.evidence: Listed on Kraken, KuCoin, Gate.io, MEXC, Bitget, Crypto.com. Not on Binance main exchange (only Binance Alpha). Daily volume ~$1.18M. 19 exchanges total. Liquidity is adequate for small-to-medium positions but thin for larger trades. Volume is in the $1-10M range but on the low end.

returns_score.returns_summary: peaq has genuine ecosystem traction — 60+ DePINs, 40M+ quarterly transactions, enterprise partnerships. The token has strong multi-utility design. But revenue is minimal ($163.6K annualised), the token is down 98% from ATH, insider allocation is heavy at 45%, and Polkadot parachain dependency adds platform risk. Returns Score 47/100 (D) reflects real activity but unproven monetisation in a nascent machine economy.

what_works_today: 60+ DePINs across 22 industries building on peaq — genuine ecosystem breadth
40.4M transactions in Q3 2025 — real on-chain activity
6M+ people and machines on-chain (primarily via Teneo)
Enterprise partnerships: NTT, Continental, Airbus, Mastercard, Bosch, Gaia
Well-engineered native machine-economy modules (peaq ID, access, verify, store, pay)
Strong audit history — CertiK 97.96/100, SRL audit completed
49K TPS with 500ms block finalisation — performant L1
what_is_hype: 6M+ machines on-chain is dominated by a single project (Teneo) — concentration risk
Enterprise partnerships listed on homepage but depth of integration unclear
$163.6K annualised fees for an L1 with 60+ DePINs is extremely low monetisation
Token down 98% from ATH — the market has not rewarded the ecosystem growth
Machine Economy narrative is compelling but commercial proof is thin
key_risks: Minimal revenue — $163.6K annualised fees does not support the chain's cost structure
Token down 98% from ATH ($0.75 to $0.015) — severe value destruction for early investors
45% insider allocation with vesting still in progress
Polkadot parachain dependency — must maintain slot, Polkadot ecosystem health matters
Ecosystem dominated by a few large DePINs (Teneo, DeNet, Silencio) — concentration risk
DePIN/Machine Economy category is nascent — unclear how large the addressable market is
Competition from Solana DePIN ecosystem and general-purpose L1s adding DePIN support
No Binance main listing limits retail discovery and liquidity
competitive_advantages: Purpose-built L1 for machine economy — native modules not available on general-purpose chains
Polkadot shared security and cross-chain interoperability via IBC/XCM
Machine identity (DID) system is unique — W3C compliant self-sovereign machine identities
Enterprise relationships (NTT, Continental, Airbus, Bosch) provide credibility
competitive_disadvantages: Polkadot ecosystem is smaller and less liquid than Ethereum or Solana
DePIN narrative has cooled from 2024 peak — less retail attention
Revenue per DePIN is extremely low — ecosystem breadth without depth
General-purpose L1s (Solana, Base, Arbitrum) are adding DePIN capabilities
Developer mindshare is lower than EVM-dominant chains
assessment.outlook: peaq has built a genuine machine-economy L1 with real ecosystem traction — 60+ DePINs, enterprise partnerships, and millions of on-chain interactions. The architecture is well-designed with native modules purpose-built for machine identity and M2M payments. But the commercial case is weak — $163.6K annualised revenue for an L1 chain is not sustainable. The token's 98% decline from ATH reflects market scepticism about DePIN monetisation. peaq needs its DePIN ecosystem to generate meaningful transaction volume that translates to fee revenue. The Machine Economy thesis is forward-looking — the question is timing, and the market is not rewarding early positioning.
assessment.overall_assessment: Well-engineered DePIN infrastructure with real ecosystem activity but minimal monetisation. Strong technical foundation, weak commercial proof. Quadrant D — moderate freedom, low returns.
